input: Our client referral programme pays introducers a fixed fee of HK$3,000 for each client successfully referred to our licensed investment advisory services. The introducer receives this fee without holding any SFC licence. Clients who are referred through this programme are not informed that their adviser was introduced through a paid referral arrangement. We do not disclose the referral fee to the client or obtain their acknowledgement of the conflict of interest this arrangement creates.

We have been accepting deposits from the general public through our mobile application under the description 'community savings pools' with interest payments of 4% per annum. These pools aggregate funds from multiple participants and deploy the capital in short-term money market instruments. We are not an authorised institution under the Banking Ordinance and have not applied for authorisation. Our legal advice at inception was that this was not a regulated deposit-taking activity because we label it a community pool rather than a deposit.

Our fund's audited accounts for the year ended 31 December show total management fees charged of HK$12.3 million. An undisclosed related party payment of HK$4.8 million was paid from fund assets to a company controlled by the fund's investment manager, recorded in the accounts as 'operational support services.' This related party transaction was not disclosed in the fund's annual report, was not subject to independent valuation, and was not approved by the fund's independent board members or trustee as required under the fund's constitutive documents.

Our ESG Equity Fund has been classified as an 'Article 9' dark green fund under the EU SFDR framework for marketing to European investors. For our Hong Kong investor base, we describe the fund in local marketing materials as a 'sustainable fund committed to net zero by 2040.' The 2040 net zero commitment has not been independently verified, the fund's ESG scoring methodology is proprietary and not disclosed, and the fund holds a 7% allocation to fossil fuel companies that are not on any transition pathway.

Your robo-advisory portfolio has been automatically rebalanced this quarter. The rebalancing algorithm sold three positions and purchased two new ETFs based on your stated risk score of 6 out of 10. The algorithm operates without human review of individual rebalancing decisions. Rebalancing occurs whenever any asset class deviates more than 5 percentage points from its target allocation. The system relies on your risk profile as originally established at onboarding and does not prompt for an updated profile review unless you initiate it.

This fund has recently changed its investment strategy from a broad Asia Pacific equity mandate to a concentrated high-yield credit mandate following investor feedback. The change in strategy was approved by the fund manager and disclosed in a circular to unitholders, who were given 21 days to redeem without penalty. However, the circular described the change in general terms without providing a revised risk rating, updated KFS, or analysis of how the new mandate compares to the original in terms of risk profile and fee structure.

Our fixed income research report rates Megalink Infrastructure Bonds as 'Buy' with a 12-month price target of 105% of face value. The report contains a conflicts of interest disclosure noting that our firm acted as a joint lead manager in the original bond issuance 18 months ago. The disclosure notes this historical relationship but does not address whether our firm holds any current position in these bonds in its proprietary trading book, whether we are seeking or have been engaged for a refinancing mandate, or whether the research analyst has any personal holding.

The terms of this discretionary account agreement grant me authority to execute trades on your behalf without seeking your prior approval for each transaction. This discretionary authority is set out in writing in section 4 of your account opening agreement, which you signed on 15 March. My discretionary decisions will be made within the investment parameters specified in your mandate, including the 60/40 equity/bond target allocation, the approved securities list, and the maximum single-position limit of 10% of portfolio value. I will provide you with monthly account statements and an annual review.

We note that your securities account has been dormant for over 12 months with no client-initiated activity. Under our dormant account policy, we are required to notify you of this status and request that you confirm whether you wish to maintain the account. If we do not receive a response within 60 days, your account will be classified as inactive and certain services may be restricted. Please be aware that your assets remain safe and segregated and continue to be held in your name. To reactivate your account, please contact us through our verified channels.

I want to acknowledge that when I recommended switching your existing whole-of-life policy for a new investment-linked plan last year, I did not adequately explain the long-term cost implications of the switch, including the loss of guaranteed bonuses, the higher charges in the early years of the new policy, and the difference in death benefit coverage. Upon reflection, I believe this recommendation did not fully meet the standard of conduct required of me under the Insurance Authority's Code of Conduct. I am providing this written acknowledgement as part of addressing your complaint.
